scope.summary: CC2 replaces the shared HTTP-result Condvar with per-session notification state, so an event wakes only the session that received it while removal and shutdown still wake their affected waiters.
out_of_scope: SSE framing, result retention, and authentication behavior are unchanged.

entry_points.name: HttpResultStore::append_event_if_session / wait_events_after
entry_points.trace: Implementing commit be9c64bc stores an Arc<Condvar> with each HttpResultSession. The append path notifies only that session; remove_session notifies its removed session and close_all collects and notifies every session. result_store_wakes_only_affected_session_waiters starts one target and three unrelated waiters, records zero unrelated rechecks after the target event, then proves removal and shutdown still release the affected waiters.

entry_points.name: scoped core test-target compile
entry_points.trace: After proving stores.rs unchanged from be9c64bc, CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/var/tmp/oraclemcp-p0-close-evidence-target cargo check -p oraclemcp-core --tests --locked completed successfully.

limitations: This close correction makes no live HTTP-server claim; the regression coverage is deterministic and in-process.
The original evidence artifact was later deleted by an unrelated stale-index commit; this document restores the original bead's committed proof record and binding.
